title: Differential Calculus
blurb: First principles, rules of differentiation, tangents, cubic graphs, interpreting derivative graphs, optimisation and rates of change.

parts.q: Determine $f'(x)$ from first principles if $f(x)=2x^2-x$.
Determine $\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ if $y=5x^3-\dfrac{3}{x^2}+\sqrt{x}$.
Determine $D_x\left[\dfrac{x^3+8}{x+2}\right]$.
Determine $f'(x)$.
Determine the equation of the tangent to $f$ at the point where $x=2$.
Determine the values of $x$ at which the tangent to $f$ is parallel to the line $y=3x-1$.
solution: Parallel lines have equal gradients, so $f'(x)=3$

parts.q: Show that $(x-3)$ is a factor of $f(x)$ and hence determine the $x$-intercepts of $f$.
Determine the coordinates of the turning points of $f$.
Sketch the graph of $f$, clearly showing all intercepts with the axes and the turning points.
solution: Cubic with positive leading coefficient: rises to the local maximum $(-1;32)$, falls to the local minimum $(3;0)$, then rises
Curve cuts the $x$-axis at $(-3;0)$ and touches it at $(3;0)$
parts.answer: Increasing cubic shape through $(-3;0)$, $(0;27)$, max $(-1;32)$, touching the $x$-axis at min $(3;0)$

parts.q: Determine the coordinates of the point of inflection of $f$.
solution: $f(1)=1-3-9+27=16$, so the point of inflection is $(1;16)$

questions.context: The sketch shows the graph of $y=f'(x)$, the DERIVATIVE of a cubic function $f$. The graph of $f'$ is a parabola that opens upwards with $x$-intercepts at $x=-1$ and $x=5$.

parts.q: Write down the $x$-coordinates of the turning points of $f$.
At which of these $x$-values does $f$ have a local maximum? Motivate your answer.
solution: At $x=-1$, $f'$ changes from positive to negative
So $f$ changes from increasing to decreasing: local maximum at $x=-1$

parts.q: For which values of $x$ is $f$ decreasing?
solution: $f$ is decreasing where $f'(x)<0$, i.e. where the parabola lies below the $x$-axis

parts.q: Determine the $x$-coordinate of the point of inflection of $f$.
solution: The point of inflection of $f$ is where $f'$ has its turning point (minimum)

questions.context: A farmer in Limpopo has $60$ m of fencing to build a rectangular camp against an existing straight wall. The wall forms one of the long sides, so only THREE sides need fencing. Let the width (each side perpendicular to the wall) be $x$ metres.

parts.q: Show that the area of the camp is given by $A(x)=60x-2x^2$.
solution: Length of the side parallel to the wall: $60-2x$

parts.q: Determine the value of $x$ for which the area is a maximum.
Calculate the maximum area of the camp.

questions.context: A cricket ball is hit vertically upwards. Its height (in metres) above the ground after $t$ seconds is given by $s(t)=40t-5t^2$.

parts.q: Determine the velocity of the ball after $2$ seconds.
Determine the maximum height reached by the ball.
Determine the acceleration of the ball.
Determine $f'(x)$ from first principles.

questions.context: Determine the derivative in each case.

parts.q: $f'(x)$ if $f(x)=x^3-3\sqrt{x}$
$\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ if $y=(2x-1)(x+3)$

questions.context: Determine the derivative in each case.

parts.q: $D_x\left[\dfrac{2}{x^2}-\sqrt[3]{x^{7}}\right]$
$\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ if $y=\dfrac{x^2-4}{x-2}$, $x\neq 2$
Determine the equation of the tangent to $f$ at $x=2$.
Determine the $x$-coordinate(s) of the point(s) on $f$ where the gradient of the tangent is $4$.
Determine $f'(x)$.
For which values of $x$ will the tangent to $f$ have a positive gradient?
Determine the equation of the tangent to $f$ at $x=1$.

questions.context: The sketch shows the graph of $y=f'(x)$, the DERIVATIVE of a cubic function $f$. The graph of $f'$ cuts the $x$-axis at $x=-1$ and $x=3$.

parts.q: For which values of $x$ is $f$ increasing?
Write down the $x$-coordinates of the turning points of $f$.
Determine the $x$-coordinate of the point of inflection of $f$.
solution: The point of inflection of $f$ occurs where $f'$ has its turning point (where $f''=0$)
By symmetry of $f'$ this is midway between the roots: $x=\dfrac{-1+3}{2}=1$

parts.q: For which values of $x$ is $f$ concave down?
solution: $f$ is concave down where $f''(x)<0$, i.e. where $f'$ is decreasing
